channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: 6 FIG HELL — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
6 FIG HELL

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-04-01 19:50 UTC

OP

HOW DO I PUSH IT TO 7 FIGS AAAAAAAAAARHHHHHHHHHH

Early replies (first 3)

62062096
>>62062044

Wait.

62062307
>>62062044
Buy accounts into prop firms until you get accepted. Learn orderflow and put all the gains on bitcoin or international stocks. You have a lot of money to lose trying to pass the prop firm test, you should be okay anon.

62062320
>>62062044
Only 2.5% of Americans have 1M.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62062341
>>62062044
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62062348
>>62062320
>25% have $2k liquid
Whelp it may not be gorillionaire but least I’m in the top 25%, bottom 75 are officially Goyim.

62062379
>>62062348
25% only 2k liquid? Wtf?

62062406
>>62062379
That’s how poor majority of people and Americans are.
Remember, the majority of the internet are people like this.

62062413
>>62062341
i was a millionaire until I got Trump'd.

62062417
>>62062413
Then you were still poor.
Get a professional wealth manager.

62062452
you just need to make more money, there's no other recourse

investing is a meme currently. expect 5-7% returns annually over the next decade. there will be no 10-15% from the insane bull market we just had unless you're willing to play with leveraged assets

62062482
>>62062406
25% is a lot. How tf they survive if something happens? If they crash the car they are done?

62062548
>>62062482
They live paycheck to paycheck

62062570
>>62062320
This isn't a good example because I would explicitly have millions if I had put it all into BTC in september 2020 when it was around 9k, taken profits at 45k a year later in december under the assumption that december was the top just following the 2017 chart. And then followed the 2017 chart again by buying in november 2018 at 20k with all I had, which would then easily have amounted to over a million in december 2025- and this is assuming I am retarded and strictly follow the 2017 bottoms and tops as a model, not even attempting to sell high and buy low.

62062579
>>62062406
if people werent retarded spenders they would have money saved
>>62062482
no they will just ask the bank to borrow even more money

62062604
>>62062482
I don't think it's true because they must have money in their 401k. I think it's mostly just a statement on how little people keep LIQUID, not in total. If you fuck yourself up and need cash obviously you have tosell investments and things to cover it.

62062610
>>62062348
Top 24% means nothing to me.
Most people have shit lives, beating that isn’t an achievement

62062619
>>62062044
Wait until you get to 7 figure purgatory. Too much money to not want to work but too little to live life

62062754
>>62062044
I fell out of 6 figures hell a few months ago and now I just want to get back

62062764
>>62062610
True. I want to live at a 5-star resort and have fun every day.

62062843
I’m making 4 figs.
t. NEET

62062848
>>62062619
Yeah.
Moral of the story.
Dont have kids if your kids are going to be struggling poor or working class.

62062993
>>62062044
>folio hit 6 figures again today.
only 10x (not adjusted for inflation) and my real life can begin.

62063154
>>62062044
I've been in 7 figure purgatory for 5 years. The humiliation doesn't stop here.

62063163
>>62062320
Wtf really? That can't be right. I'm at like $1.4m total nw and I'm only 35. Surely most boomers with paid off houses and retirement accounts are over $1m.

62063293
>>62063163
im in a similar position. apparently thats top 5% nw for mid-30s. doesn't feel like it.

62063412
I have been stuck at 400-470k for the past 3 years since I lost my online income. Im a NEET now.
I had a peak at 670k with MSTR but took a screenshot instead of gains. And im also bagholding 2 stocks (BTBT and IREN). Now im considering sweet cide.

62063597
>>62062341
You're a nigger

62064061
>>62063163
houses don't really count...if you live in a desirable area, no hoa, low tax state, and check all the boxes so to speak, you can use like 50-70% of the "zestimate" as the worth value for a building of equivalent sqft. It's a little harsh when they say dont use home value at all, but if it's paid off you at a minimum have the land value minus demolition and selling costs. On the other hand, most people are completely delusional about "improvements" they've made to a house.

62064082
Can any of your rich fags help me with 400 to invest I wanna get our of the shithole am in eventually

btc 15A3Y7sA1f9ABkqg7jyka8evhhKo7324T2

62064089
>>62064082
>>62062096
>>62062619
>>62062754
>>62063154
Help a newfag out anons

62064188
>>62064089
Bump

62064218
Gambling :)

62064226
>>62062044
No joke. You need to start your own business to get to the next class

item.title: KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED — 23 replies — 47.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-04-01 19:35 UTC

OP

Ripple is partnering with Convera to expand enterprise cross-border payments.

Together, they’re combining global payment rails with stablecoin-enabled settlement to improve speed, liquidity, and reliability.

>https://x.com/Ripple/status/2038969816187285516 (not fake or clickbait)

Early replies (first 3)

62057459
>>62057451
So bullish

62057468
>>62057451
>Convera

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62057502
Bullish xrp

62057599
uhh can i get a

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057946
>>62057599
checked and BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057993
>>62057451
>partnering
>enterprise
>cross-border payments
>global payment rails
>stablecoin-enabled settlement
>speed
>liquidity
>reliability

62058568
>>62057993
BOOOOOOOOOOOOZZWORRDSSMM!

62058935
>>62057451
umm embarrassing....?

62058947
BOOM BOOOM BOOM

62061203
BOOOOOOOM

62061366
Keep seething, linkmarines

62061409
Con

62061530
>>62057451
Not buying your heavy bags kike

62062460
>>62058935
>>62057468
These niggers cant google KEEEK

Originally known as Western Union Business Solutions before its 2021 acquisition, Convera reported over USD 190 billion in turnover in 2025 and serves more than 26,000 businesses worldwide. Headquartered in Seattle, it employs 1,001-5,000 people and focuses on tech-driven solutions for FX, compliance, and risk management.

62062479
>>62057451
>leveraging regulated stablecoins for settlement

XRP not needed lol

62062595
>Good news for xrp
>dumps
Never gets old

62062632
>>62062595
It’s literally pumping right now linktard

62062694
>>62058947
I know this is AI, because the backblast would have blown down the door and severely concussed those cats.

62062728
>>62062595
>>Good news for xrp
*good news for Ripple shareholders, not for xrp.

>>62062479

62063129
>>62057451
BOOOOOOOOOOM

62064107
>>62057451
BOOOOOOM

62064147
SO

FXCKING
BULLISH

item.title: I CAN'T BREAK INTO THE CAREER I KNOW I SUCCEED AT — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I CAN'T BREAK INTO THE CAREER I KNOW I SUCCEED AT

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-01 20:03 UTC

OP

I CAN'T BREAK INTO THE CAREER I KNOW I SUCCEED AT

Early replies (first 3)

62056566
>>62056560
which one?

62056596
>>62056566
industrial automation engineer

62056636
>>62056596
why did you graduate without experience?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62056666
>>62056560
Im about to pay 60k starting this summer to pay professors to get me the job interview with the appropriate government institution for my career to finally take off.

I feel really dumb, but long term this investment is a positive.

62061884
>>62056560
https://www.youtube.com/@beeautomation

62061969
>>62056666
kek retard you got got

62061973
>>62061969
nahhh their own website says 96% of students go straight into employment!
They wouldnt lie to me when I pay them so much money

62063446
>>62056636
because when i graduated Industrial Engineering, I didn't know what the fuck i wanted to do. I work as a quality engineer for a while then as a meme sales engineer, but along the line i realised that industrial automation perfectly matches my skills (computers and hardware) and resources (Bachelor's in Industrial Engineering).

62063508
>>62056560
relateable
>>62056636
>just know how everything works at 19 bro
i wish i had worked for a few years and gone to school at ~23 instead of at 18
>>62063446
if you can break into it then that should be a decent line of work
engineering and industry fucking sucks ass here and is full of dinosaurs nesting on all the decent jobs despite the fact they can't even do them anymore; it's sufficiently arcane that nobody inside can tell that they can't do it and nobody dares replace them either
>>62056666
a foot in the door is basically all academic institutions even provide - the actual education is shit and largely useless
quads confirm you're doing the right thing anon

62064283
>>62056560
just beeeee urself <3

62063327
>>62063269
Holy crap! $10000 dollars today will make me 10 million by EOY? How is no one talking about this one in a lifetime investment??

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62063369

My cousin has been talking about it for a year now. Guess how he's doing right now?

62063383

What did I tell you about talking anout be on the board, Ned!?

62063384
>>62063269
holy fuck someone is good troll or retarded

62064269

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 230 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 230 • Updated: 2026-04-01 14:19 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62048431
At least 119 of 200 posts are by OP. This should violate a rule.

62048708
>>62045475
You mean there are none?
>>62048431
Obamaleaf was worse

62048850
>>62048431
>You mean there are none?
What would you regard as "influential news"?

62048939
>>62048850
What do you think? Can you define influential

62048959
>>62048939
>Can you define influential
No, because it's a subjective word. To some people a towns hall meting of a town of 500 people might be the most "influential" thing in the world since it affect them, their home town and their lives. To someone else it's a completely irrelevant event because it doesn't affect anyone or anything in their own lives. To some people the war in Iran is completely unimportant and doesn't affect them. To some people, like the Iranians is a life changing event.

The word "influential" news is in the perception of the beholder. I can give you as much stuff as you want, it's just... If you don't care about it then it's not "influental", that's why I'm asking
>What would you regard as "influential news"?
Since every individual themselves need to determine what THEY think is influential for them and tickles their funny bone.

62049007
>>62048939
Here, a google definiton; Influential describes
someone or something with the power to cause change, shape decisions, or exert significant impact on people or events. It implies having high status, authority, or being highly important (e.g., an influential leader or book).

62049042
>>62048939
>>62045442
>>62048708
If you can't even define what you want then how can I even give it to you, right? I can only give you something you can actually define.

62049654
>>62048959
>>62049007
>>62049042

You literally shill this fucking coin. Do some influencing

62051513
>>62049654
Yes. Define "influencing". What would INFLUENCE YOU? Naked ladies? Hackatons? Drugs? New technical developments? What influences YOU? If I just post something then you'll be like "No, that's not good enough", so what's the actual point if I can't give you EXACTLY what you want?

62051763
>>62051513
From reading the thread I thought you had some intellectual insight, nope an idiot

62051818
>>62051763
>From reading the thread I thought you had some intellectual insight
You tell me what you think is an "intellectual insight"? What would convince you? Throwing a hissy fit and getting emotional at me doesn't really work...

62052015
>>62051763
I'm not sure if I'm making myself clear enough. Define what you think would constitute for something that YOU consider an "intellectual insight" in your own mind? Can you post a piece of information from another chain that amazed you, that made you think, that made you say "Oh shit, this is lit"? I hope I've made myself clear enough good sar.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056424
>>62051763
100$ end of bull run. Here, some influencing for you.


Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056570
>>62051763
>>62049654
>>62048939
>>62048708
>>62045442
Here you go. Let me influence you young Vishnus of the mindchain; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gg4P23PirgU
DOT will become the Web3 cloud. It'll literally be able to do work for ETH, which means it'll be able to scale ETH to millions of TPS. ETH will be able to forward transactions to DOT and DOT will be able to execute them for ETH therefore scaling ETH to millions of TPS. DOT will be able to do that for any arbitrary project (not sure about stuff like BTC and DOGE that has no smart contracts. Maybe that too, yet I can't comment on that because I really don't know at this point).

62056612
>>62056570
>DOT will become the Web3 cloud. It'll literally be able to do work for ETH, which means it'll be able to scale ETH to millions of TPS. ETH will be able to forward transactions to DOT and DOT will be able to execute them for ETH therefore scaling ETH to millions of TPS. DOT will be able to do that for any arbitrary project (not sure about stuff like BTC and DOGE that has no smart contracts. Maybe that too, yet I can't comment on that because I really don't know at this point).
This is JUST one of the possible applications. Of course you'll be able to execute ANY arbitrary operation on-chain, like any Web2 cloud can. Imagine a WHOLE GAME deployed on-chain. Literally every step of your character, every emote, every chat message, every loot drop and pick up being on-chain. You'll of course also be able to run AI agents and shit like that... DOT is the ONLY chain capable of doing MILLIONS of TPS once NOMT + JAM get implemented
NOMT alone will x10 the throughput of the whole DOT chain. https://www.rob.tech/blog/introducing-nomt/ and https://github.com/thrumdev/nomt and https://www.reddit.com/r/Polkadot/comments/1nzjhuq/what_has_happened_to_nomt/ and https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N3BX2eTqpro

So what's getting shipped this year that's considered "influential" for out young Vishnus of the mindchain? JAM, NOMT, the Polkadot App and more stuff (instant unstaking (unstaking will go from 28 days do 1-2 days)), project individuality (proof of personhood that doesn't require KYC, 500ms block (Basti blocks), and more)

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056623
How do I buy this shit and how long do I hold

62056631
When will you incels realize shitcoins only go up if bitcoin goes up, it doesn’t matter how much ‘bullish’ news a shitcoin gets

62056642
>>62056623
What do you mean "How do I buy this shit"? Bro, if this comes of as mean, it's not meant as mean and I'm sorry if it sounds mean. If you can't figure out how to buy something yourself then please just don't buy it at all. Make yourself a favor

>>62056631
You do realize that this won't be true forever? And that certain projects do decouple from BTC when they're ripe for it.

62056686
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gg4P23PirgU

Oliver makes a really good point here where he tries to point out that like 4 years ago L2s could just exist and people would use them and NOW people are demanding that the L2s upgrade and improve in order to still be considered useful. At this point I would like to add an additional thing. The thing that people want from major L2s (like Arbitrum and AVAX and other stuff like that) is what DOT L2s already have. The major L2s can't compete with DOT because they're behind and once JAM hits they'll be even more behind. In the time DOT gets JAM the L2s won't even have what DOT has RIGHT NOW. DOT has shared security, it has built-in native SAFE bridging (that's not even a bridge, it's a XCM, it uses HRMP channels), because of the shared security it DOES NOT fragment liquidity, you can use the functionality of ANOTHER chain on DOT (so of other DOT chains and other chains in general that aren't on DOT). Once people realize that they can EASILY build on DOT while every other chains LIMITS them in some shape or form they'll start flocking to DOT. Every other chain has a ton of problems that DOT simply DOES NOT therefore DOT is technologically speaking superior. Sooner or later the developers will catch onto it and start developing on DOT since the other chains have limited capabilities. You can only develop humdrum applications on ETH, no innovation will ever come out of ETH ever again. ETH is obselete and unreconcilable. Sure it'll stay relevant for a few more year but how much longer can it stand the test of time? BTC is windows 1.0, ETH is windows 98, DOT is Windows 12 lol (Yes, not Windows 11, it's windows 12).

62056998
>>62056631
The game is changing. This bear market will prove it

62057110
>>62056998
>The game is changing.
What do you see anon? Help us understand what you see when you say "The game is changing". And yes, I do agree that stuff will shift from stupidity back to usability. So, meme coins were the LAST DROP. Enough is ENOUGH. We need to go back to our roots and invest into stuff that actually makes sense and is GROUND BREAKING. If we don't pivot in this liminal time then the industry as a whole will be finished for like 10-20 years UNTIL we pivot there is no way forward. We need to REALIZE that BTC is crap, ETH is crap, SOL is crap, USDT is centralized shit, XRP is useless, TRX is a cult run piece of shit, lol BCH is in the top 10 WTF, DOGE is crap, BNB is fucking crap. It's all just useless junk. Literally the whole of top 10 is fucking garbage that's unusable. It's literally all Windows 1 and Windows 98 type of coins while we already have the Vishnu of the mindchain, the Windows 12 of blockchains, the collectibe 1/9 Lamborghini Veneno Roadster of the blockchain, the fucking Rolls-Royce of the blockchain industry, and we're still stuck with the fucking Windows 1 and Windows 98 of the blockchain industry. Guys, it's time for FUCKING CHANGE. Either we pivot to the 3rd generation blockchains or the whole sector will GO UNDER for a decade or two. Do you want to suck cock for 10 to 20 years? Or do you just market sell your garbage gen 1 and gen 2 tokens/coins and move on with your life and buy the actual technical innovation? It's choice time and it's GOOD to know that any day you can change your whole fucking life. If you want the world to change then the first step to changing the world is to CHANGE YOUR FUCKING SELF. You only need to 1. WANT THE CHANGE and 2. BELIEVE YOU CAN CHANGE (Self-efficacy). Those are the 2 most important principles of BEHAVIOURAL CHANGE. Change your behaviour and with that you'll change the world. Sure it'll take time and effort but hey, even wallowing in your misery, being depressed takes time and effort.

62058962
>>62056631
Algorand is pumping while Bitcoin is dumping because it is quantum secure. Nigger

62059225
DOT will reach the $1.60 range before this week ends

62059268
>>62057110
See was that so hard on being influential?

62060540
>>62059268
The thing is, that "influenced" you... Doesn't mean it influenced the other people. Everyone is a unique little program and therefore needs unique inputs in order to produce the desired output. One man's trash is another man's treasure

>>62058962
Nagger

62060621
100$ end of the FUCKING bull run. Here's some influential and intellectual insights

62060648
>>62060540
How do you know. Besides the intent of you making this thread was to educate/shill/ influence the mass retards on this site, don't try to convince me otherwise

62062169
>>62060648
>How do you know.
What do you mean by that? How do I know what?

62062500
>>62060648
How I know that people are unique little programs and that I need to input unique inputs to get the desired outputs? Is that what you're asking or?

62062550
Let me "influence" you young Vishnus of the MINDchain. Listen to one of our spiritual leaders Oliver Talé-Yaźdi how he talks about the Vishnu mindchain. Join our cult *kmh* *kmh* web3 organization where we talk about the new world order.

https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2039256751346700502

>$8
you're a retarded cuckold

62048655

62051982


62054375
fuck em all so much
hate em all think they're dumb so gay too
hate all stinks
fuckin cringe ass niggas

62054396
>>62054375

62054429
>>62015802

62054451
>>62054429

>Waking up every day feeling high and mighty

62054546
>>62045180

not even trying to fud. no clarity act wont increase hype. and no banks are not trying to surpress price.

there is no hype and no demand. no community ,

62054560

62054650

62054880

just 20 more years until we get back to all time high!

item.title: What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday. — 79 replies — 23.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-04-01 16:42 UTC

OP

What the fuck is going on with MSFT?
How is this not a great entry?
Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Early replies (first 3)

62043952
because the music has stopped

fucking cretin

62043962
Just buy gold and gold mining stocks.

62043971
>>62043947
I would wait until i see signs of of absolute panic.
And even then only go in for a medium term trade.
Liquidity is not great and there are big IPOs coming that will absorb even more of it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62050570
I work as a Dynamics365 software developer. Do you guys think is time to switch to another career?

62050633
>>62043947
Nobody has ever gone broke betting on Microsoft. They are a shit company, but the entire world runs on Excel spreadsheets.

62050873
>>62043947
Just do it. This is a temporary economic crisis almost at the same level as covid. You can set yourself up for a great situation once the dust settles. I was a millionaire after the crash in 2020 because I never stopped my DCA and I loaded up lumps sums of cash into tech stocks and BTC while everyone here was posting pinkjaks.

62050883
>>62043947
Hey, I work at Microsoft as a Senior Data Center Technician. It's an absolute mess. We're have soooo many data centers going up, including an OpenAI project, but with the cracks clearly showing in OpenAI's profitability, as well as Sora getting shut down, there is a lot of tension in the air. We just got a hiring freeze nationwide, if I'm not mistaken. Our network engineers are all outsourced to India. We do not have an on-site NOC at any of our facilities, as far as I'm aware. These street shitter network engineers are completely incompetent and projects drag because of them. In addition, dIvERsiTy iZ oUr StrEngt here, so a lot of new techs we hire are incompetent brown/black trash. One manager just hired his son. I'm probably going to be quitting the data center world once this bubble pops, which is most likely this year.

62051391
>>62043947
Because when you go all in on AI, you lose. Shrimple as.

62051399
Great entry for a short

62051403
>>62050633
No one went broke betting on the turkey before thanksgiving

62051497
>>62043947
the company is jeeted to a point where you have to wonder if they can ever get back from that

62051499
Biz as always, low IQ people blaming everything on indians. Like if MICROSOFT hasn't been shit forever. But now, it's all indians fault. Sure buddies. Fucking retards

62051760
>>62049635
>>62049655
You're burying the lead. The level of goodwill they lost from the Xbone cannot be understated. They were saying insane shit like the kinect would be able to count how many people were in your living room watching a movie, and the rental price would dynamically change to accommodate it. And you wouldnt even be able to lend your game discs out, theyd be hard linked to your console.

Only for Sony to hit the stage at the next E3 and announce a "dynamic" new way to lend your game to friends. Only for one exec to walk up, then cheekily hand his ps4 disc to another exec. And some shit about all processing being handled in "the cloud", which gamers immediately saw through as Oh we wont really own our games theyll be on Microshit's servers forever. It was a fucking debacle all around. Even the price, Xbone was $499, PS4 was $399.

62051770
”we support bipoc trans gamers” - minecraft title screen after jeetsoft acquisition

62051787
>>62043947
In those 3 peaks I see
>exuberance
>metered enthusiasm that is beginning to wane
>desperation
where we are now, it might try to rechallenge the previous high, but it will fall short and then we'll start to see the 'exposé' articles hit the news after it starts plummeting
I have no position in ms, just how I see it going down

62051789
>>62051499

>Microsoft was always a mixed bag
>now there are Indians on top of that

how does this not make sense to you

62051794
The company is failing because it is too jeeted. The cheap labor pumped up the stock to astronomically high levels. But now the effects of that are lowering the value of the company product. So the sock has to go down as investors look to alternatives for better products.

62051822
>>62051497
Usually when the rot (Indians or other nepo groups) gets past a certain points its just irreversibly fucked.
Not a collaspe by any means but just slowly fading into irrelevance and incompetence like the legacy tech companies (IBM, HPE, whatever)

62051849
>>62051822

IBM is the best example. It was huge. America's first premier tech company. It should still be so. But nope. They were the first to take the mass Indianification poison pill, greedy execs looking for cheaper workers, until it backfired and the Indian clan had enough collective power to take it over entirely. Now it's just an eternally irrelevant dinosaur and they squat on the embers collecting whatever spare pennies they can off of it.

62051889
>>62051499
msft was garbage first, but they improved and XP and later Win7 were kinda ok, but then sudenly they started implementing jeets and everything went to shit

62051895
>>62043947
MacroHard gonna pummel Microjeet like a Pajeeta girl on her first night off the plane in Texas getting pounded by big white cock.

62052017
>>62049474
and it still isn’t worth it to use

62052128
>>62043947

It’s because they attached themselves to the hip to openAI. Same as oracle, market knows Altman is a scammer

62053062
>>62048780
Azure is also trash, even open AI is trying to switch to AWS and they opened a law suit against them.

62053066
>>62049594
>They deserve to die for what they did to Halo
This.

62054681
>>62050175
sybau

62054695
>>62043947
No Indian company should be worth 2.67T

62054705
>>62047799
Remember that firmware update from Microsoft a few months back that supposedly was a bug that fried few peoples SSDs?
Good times

62054994
>>62051399
no it isnt, you shouldve shorted months ago lol now its a long ish wait 2-3 weeks for hammer fists

62056532
>>62054695
True.
And everyone hates America now.
Many are moving away from Microjeet and Azure.

62061658
>>62047766
DIS NIGGA USIN ICT LMAO
DOW Theory for zoomers

62061676
>>62043947
>since this post 4 days ago, it has began a head-n-shoulders off support at 350
Good luck believers.

62063135
>>62049970
there's an indicator that does that automatically

item.title: Idiot — 17 replies — 16.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Idiot

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-01 19:00 UTC

OP

I opened a BTC trade at 105k, ask me anything

Early replies (first 3)

62057597
>>62057584
are u ok?

62057606
>>62057597
No.

62057621
>>62057606
:(

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62057676
>>62057584
this is a slurping year. you should average down. and ride it back up. you'll need 5 BTC to retire by 2035 so don't waste this opportunity. you are still buying, right?

62057686
>>62057676
If you're betting on the next 10 years, why buy now? Why not wait until October when it will be $40-50k?

62057805
>>62057686
Because le heckin accurate 4 year cycle is known to everyone by now so we might not see it dip that low. We are already below the previous ATH which is an anomaly by itself.

62057900
>>62057686
>why not time the bottom?
it never feels like the bottom at the bottom so i just buy on a schedule stress free.

62057917
>>62057597
BUSTAH WOLF

62057920
>>62057900
Buying on a schedule isn't much better than trading based on gut feeling of what "feels" like the bottom.

62057969
>>62057920
you must have a trustworthy gut. good for you.
the closer we get to the 200w ma the more i buy.

it works for me to subtract emotion and just make it a numbers game. i don't buy when the price is extended 25% over the 200w ma.

62058042
>>62057969
Track liquidity maps and chain flows. You'll know the bottom is in when the sellers have run out of stock.
Or just ask chatgpt or whatever to do it for you. It'll probably lag behind by an hour or so, but they're pretty capable of giving a decent summary.

62058162
>>62057584
how often does your lender visit and is lube an option?

62060158
>>62057597
>>62057606
he's a godtrader.

62060754
>>62057917
KEK, based and terry-pilled

62060771
>>62057917
Kek

62060786
>>62057584
Have a complementary F

62063985
Make it all back in one trade

CreditScoreProtocol * com

OP

Its literally over for stinkers.

Ripple Treasury is officially attending Swift Sibos 2026. While the world talks about the future of finance, Ripple is building it. Ripple will be showcasing how XRP and Ripple are transforming global liquidity.

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

62063071

62063237

62063538

item.title: >Choose Life. Choose a job. Choose a career. Choose a family. Choose a fucking big television, choose washing machines,… — 37 replies — 9.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Choose Life. Choose a job. Choose a career. Choose a family. Choose a fucking big television, choose washing machines,…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-04-01 16:14 UTC

OP

>Choose Life. Choose a job. Choose a career. Choose a family. Choose a fucking big television, choose washing machines, cars, compact disc players and electrical tin openers. Choose good health, low cholesterol, and dental insurance. Choose fixed interest mortgage repayments. Choose a starter home. Choose your friends. Choose leisurewear and matching luggage. Choose a three-piece suit on hire purchase in a range of fucking fabrics. Choose DIY and wondering who the fuck you are on Sunday morning. Choose sitting on that couch watching mind-numbing, spirit-crushing game shows, stuffing fucking junk food into your mouth. Choose rotting away at the end of it all, pissing your last in a miserable home, nothing more than an embarrassment to the selfish, fucked up brats you spawned to replace yourselves. Choose your future.

>Choose life... But why would I want to do a thing like that? I chose not to choose life. I chose somethin' else. And the reasons? There are no reasons. Who needs reasons when you've got heroin?

Early replies (first 3)

62060931
do heroin addicts really think theyre above normies

62060938
>>62060923
is this some quote from a book. I dont get it frogposter

62060941
>>62060938
I think it's song lyrics

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62061075
>>62061074
Good, so tired of movies ALWAYS needing to end with a wholesome twisterino. Especially when movies try to seem cool and edgy

62061082
>>62060923
Trainspotting. Great film.

62061086
>>62061009
It's a direct quote. It's a personal favorite film of mine, I've heard this line countless times.

62061102
I choose the president is trump! You can cope.

62061112
The Zone is a very complicated system of traps, and they're all deadly. I don't know what goes on here when people are absent, but the moment someone shows up, everything comes into motion. Old traps disappear and new ones emerge. Safe spots become impassable. One minute your path is easy, the next it's hopelessly convoluted. That's the Zone. It may seem capricious, but it is what we've made it with our condition. I've seen people stop halfway and go back. Some of them even died on the very threshold of the Room. But everything that happens here depends not on the Zone, but on us!
>So it lets the good ones pass and kills the bad ones?
I don't know... I think it lets those pass who have lost all hope. Not good or bad, but miserable people. But even the most miserable will die if they don't know how to behave. You were lucky. It only gave you a warning.

62061404
>>62061075
The protagonist chooses to live his life instead of escaping with heroin. It IS a wholesome twistereno ending my goy

62061531
>>62061404
Ah frick...fuck movies

62061559
>>62060923

62061566
>>62060931
>Are you happy? I am, man
>Content and fully aware
>Money, status, nothing to me
'
>Cause your life is empty and bare, yeah
>You can't understand a user's mind
>But try with your books and degrees
>If you let yourself go and opened your mind
>I'll bet you'd be doing like me
>And it ain't so bad
Yes

62061642
>>62061559
>choose life

62061743
>>62061559

the cause is a lifetime worth of poopstains

62061764

62061998

62062073

62062076

62062080
>>62061074

62062113

62062117

62062272
>>62061073

62062301
>>62061112
I watched Stalker at the very end of a LSD trip. It was absolutely amazing.

62062410
>>62061057
>>62060923
Great movie. Kinda makes being a piece of shit look fun, ngl, but I know in reality that lifestyle is actually fucking horrible

62062423
>>62060923

62062431
>>62060931

62062434
>>62062410
The lifestyle is based, self destruction is based. Only real shrigmas get it. So sick of moralfags and their cookiecutter ways. Laying in a ditch dying is based would do it again no questions asked.

62062435

62062857
>>62062434

I had a friend who likes that lifestyle. I remember he told me once "rock bottom is a challenge for me". He is a fucked up dude, reminded me of the Savage from Brave New World, he lived like some kinda drug addicted modern savage man

62062916

62062933

It's really not that bad. Hanging out with your mates everyday getting high on heroin whilst being financially supported by the tax payer.

62062983
>>62062857

62062996
>>62060923
